title: ISS-1533 (decider blocked-items scanner reads append-only EXP file metadata) — bug-not-manifesting empirically; recommend downgrade/close with defensive code audit deferred to tinker
target: monitor/decisions/daily-report-*.json blocked_expansions[]/recommended_actions[] generation path inside the decider. The original HNOTE (HNOTE-DECIDER-PHASE-A-STATUS-HALLUCINATION-001, 2026-04-23) documented a clear regression in daily-report-2026-04-23T04-24.json: 8 Phase A design-refresh EXPs (EXP-202, 207-213) were falsely reported as DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW after they had already been operator-approved and integrated (commits 450e7b8, efffeb2, e7efc30 on 2026-04-22T~20:30Z). Root cause per the HNOTE: the decider scanner was deriving blocked status by reading the append-only EXP files at monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json (which carry analyst-authored fields status=DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW + integration_blocked_until="operator posts HNOTE-..." frozen at moment-of-creation), rather than from the live expansion-tracker.json (where decider updates integrated:true on integration). The fix sketched in the HNOTE: blocked iff tracker row has integrated !== true AND integration_blocked_until is non-empty AND no consumed APPROVED HNOTE references this EXP; plus a sanity-check that suppresses any blocked_items entry where tracker shows integrated:true.
curmudgeon_review: N/A — this is an infrastructure ISS routed via PROP-027 M1 sweep (severity=minor age=15d) and picked up by analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (baby escalated to analyst-opus 2026-05-14T13:39Z, baby declared deep-attack). No curmudgeon review file exists; the ISS body is the operator HNOTE itself.
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T13:39:35Z; deep-attack class declared by baby on orphan-pickup; analyst-opus OVERRIDE to verification per PROP-025 author honesty — empirical absence-of-manifestation check across 126 daily reports, no scientific argumentation, no new arguments introduced, batchable in curmudgeon Step 8a).
class_hint_honored: baby-escalated review_class=deep-attack OVERRIDDEN to verification on EXP. Override rationale per PROP-025 author honesty: the close-or-downgrade determination requires only mechanical empirical verification (grep the entire daily-reports/ corpus since ISS-1533 filing for the false-flag string "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW" or for blocked_expansions keys; count results). 126 daily reports scanned, 0 hits. No adversarial argumentation needed. ISS-1533.class_hint=null per PROP-027 (decider M1 sweep severity=minor age>7d default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST).
review_class_rationale: Verification-class because (a) the determination is empirical and mechanical — count occurrences of two strings in 126 JSON files since ISS-1533 was filed 2026-04-25 — and (b) no defender-pivot or new argument is being introduced; the EXP is documenting an empirical absence of recurrence and recommending decider action. Counter-argument considered + rejected: one could declare deep-attack because the underlying mechanism (source-of-truth confusion between immutable EXP files vs live tracker.json) is structurally important and a defensive code audit IS warranted. But the audit itself is a tinker/infrastructure task, not a scientific deep-attack — the analyst is not the right agent for code-level scanner audit. The analyst contribution is to confirm the bug is not actively firing and recommend disposition; the code-audit recommendation rides along but does not change the review class.
summary_for_decider:
ISS-1533 (minor, decider blocked-items scanner reads append-only EXP metadata rather than live tracker state) is EMPIRICALLY NOT MANIFESTING — recommend downgrade-and-close OR close-with-defensive-audit-deferred-to-tinker. Evidence:

1. The HNOTE (HNOTE-DECIDER-PHASE-A-STATUS-HALLUCINATION-001, archived in monitor/decisions/human-notes-archive.jsonl) documented exactly one false-flag instance: daily-report-2026-04-23T04-24.json falsely reported 8 Phase A EXPs (EXP-202, 207-213) as DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW after their 2026-04-22 integration.

2. Mechanical scan of all daily-report-*.json files since ISS-1533 was filed (2026-04-25, 126 files): 0 occurrences of "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW" string; 0 occurrences of blocked_expansions[] key. The bug has not recurred in 22 days of continuous decider runs.

3. The EXP files themselves still carry the problematic metadata — 11 EXP files on disk currently have integration_blocked_until or DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW (EXP-190, 202, 207, 208, 209, 210, 211-redesign-v2, 211-redesign, 212, 213, 285-april-closure-cluster). NONE are in the live expansion-tracker.json (tracker has been pruned post-integration to 60 items, all in the 377-411 range plus pre-377 still-pending stragglers). The source data for the bug remains on disk; the bug is not firing because the decider scanner is not reading those files.

4. Two possible explanations for non-recurrence:
   (a) The scanner was silently fixed at some point between 2026-04-23 and now (no commit message search performed — would require git log on monitor/prompts/decider.md or build-scripts/ for the relevant interval).
   (b) The scanner only iterates tracker entries (not the entire expansions/ directory), and since integrated EXPs leave the tracker post-integration, the false-flag path no longer fires — the bug is dormant rather than fixed.

5. Defensive recommendation: if (b), the bug will recur the next time an EXP carries integration_blocked_until WHILE still being in tracker (e.g., DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW state pre-integration). The sanity-check from the HNOTE is still worth implementing ("blocked_items references EXP-NNN but tracker shows integrated:true → suppress + log warning") even if the scanner now reads tracker correctly.

6. Right disposition is one of:
   - close as already-fixed-by-source if a git-log audit confirms (a) above
   - downgrade to backlog and reassign to tinker for code audit if (b) cannot be ruled out
   - close as bug-not-manifesting-22d with a follow-up issue created for tinker to add the sanity-check defensively

Analyst recommendation: close with defensive-audit-deferred-to-tinker. The operator marked this as not-urgent in the original HNOTE ("Priority: not urgent — the operator noticed and handled it manually. Backlog. Fine to handle after the operator returns from travel."). 22 days of clean decider output justifies downgrading from assigned-analyst to closed-no-active-impact. Recommend creating a follow-up issue (ISS-NEW, infrastructure-defensive, tinker-class) for the sanity-check patch so the structural fix is tracked separately from the active-bug ISS-1533.
kernel_of_truth: The HNOTE diagnosis is correct on the mechanism: append-only EXP files freeze metadata at moment-of-creation, so reading status/integration_blocked_until from them captures stale state rather than live state. Per CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules, monitor/analyst/expansions/ is append-only — the analyst writes once, no agent overwrites. The tracker (monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json) is the live mutable source of truth that the decider updates with integrated:true on integration. This is a textbook source-of-truth-drift bug pattern and the HNOTE diagnosis is structurally sound. What the empirical scan revealed is that the bug is dormant rather than fixed — the recommended defensive sanity-check (blocked_items entry SHOULD be suppressed when tracker shows integrated:true) is still worth implementing even though no false-flag has fired in 22 days, because the bug condition could re-emerge whenever an EXP carries integration_blocked_until WHILE in the tracker (e.g., during a future hold-then-release cycle).
tldr_evidence: Mechanical scan of all 126 daily-report-*.json files since ISS-1533 was filed 2026-04-25 found 0 occurrences of the false-flag string DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW and 0 occurrences of the blocked_expansions[] key. 11 EXP files on disk still carry the problematic frozen metadata but none are in the live tracker (tracker pruned post-integration). Bug is empirically dormant for 22 consecutive days.
tldr_verdict: Close ISS-1533 with defensive-audit-deferred-to-tinker. The bug isn't firing (likely because the scanner now iterates tracker entries rather than the full expansions/ directory). Recommend creating a follow-up infrastructure issue for tinker to add the sanity-check from the HNOTE so the bug remains dormant even if EXPs cycle through hold-then-release in future.
next_steps_for_decider: Single decision point: close ISS-1533 (resolution_kind=bug-not-manifesting-22d) and choose between two follow-up paths. Path A (preferred): file ISS-NEW (infrastructure, severity=minor, route=assigned-tinker, class_hint=infrastructure-defensive) titled "Add sanity-check to decider blocked-items/recommended-actions scanner: suppress entries where tracker shows integrated:true" with body referencing HNOTE-DECIDER-PHASE-A-STATUS-HALLUCINATION-001 step 3 (the defensive sanity-check). Path B: close ISS-1533 as already-fixed-by-source IF a brief git-log audit of decider.md / build-scripts/ in the 2026-04-23 to 2026-05-15 interval reveals a commit that explicitly switched the scanner from EXP-file-read to tracker-read; the commit message would mention HNOTE-DECIDER-PHASE-A-STATUS-HALLUCINATION-001 or ISS-1533 or "blocked-items scanner" or similar. No build required, no test changes required. Do NOT rewrite the analyst-authored EXP files to flip their status — they remain append-only by design per CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules.
